name: Crawfish Mashed Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the potatoes in a pot and cover with water. Season the water with salt.
2. Bring the potatoes up to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Simmer until fork tender (about 8-10 minutes).
3. Remove from heat and drain. Return the drained potatoes to the pot and return to heat.
4. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, to dehydrate the potatoes.
5. In a sauté pan, melt butter.
6. Sauté the potatoes in melted butter for 2 minutes.
7. Stir in minced garlic and chopped chives. Season crawfish with Essence.
8. Using a hand-held masher, mash the potatoes with remaining butter. Fold in crawfish and continue mashing.
9. Add enough cream to achieve desired texture. Season mashed potatoes with salt and white pepper.
10. Spoon sauces onto bottom of plate. Use knife to swirl sauces together. Mound mashed potatoes in center of sauce.
11. Lay fish on top of potatoes. Garnish with fried herb salad, parsley, and peppers.
Yield: 4 servings
